title: Dashboard responsive redesign — three breakpoint layouts
description:
Redesign the dashboard to support three distinct responsive breakpoints:
1. Phone (<640px): Single column, stacked widgets, compact
2. Tablet-in-hand (640-1024px): Two-column grid, medium density
3. Tablet-on-wall (>1024px): Optimized for reading at arm's length — large typography, high contrast, clear information hierarchy

The wall-mounted tablet is the north star use case. Widgets should show: upcoming tasks, today's events, shopping list status, next buses.

Acceptance criteria:
- Dashboard looks good and is usable at all three breakpoints
- Wall-tablet layout emphasizes glanceability — readable at 3-4 feet
- Typography scales appropriately per breakpoint

Dev Lead.notes: Full dashboard rewrite with three breakpoints. Wall-tablet layout leads with clock, highlights overdue tasks, color-coded user pills. Clean extraction of widget components. TypeScript passes.

Customer.notes: Customer tested on ZFold 7 — two columns unfolded, one column folded, breakpoints working correctly. Approved as shipped (meeting 2026-03-25).

userVote.notes: Verified on ZFold 7 and 1080p TV via mini computer. Looks good.
